# On the user — Cassian Vire

<!-- Slow-growing picture of the user. Only update when something non-obvious comes up. No timestamps — this is a synthesized portrait, not a log. -->

Rejects strategic voting as a standing principle — not a casual preference, an articulated commitment. Worth noting because it changes how political recommendations should be framed: the question is always fit, never electability math.

EU federalism is load-bearing for this user, not decorative. It sits at the same tier as democratic values and digital rights. Any future political or governance question should treat European integration depth as a first-order variable, not background colour.

SMR-positive. The nuclear stance is considered, not reflexive — pairs with the systems-level thinking pattern generally.
